candidates.title: CV Predicts Steering Heterogeneity Within Absorbed SAE Features
candidates.summary: CV-based decomposition of absorbed features using median CV split (prospective, not post-hoc CV > 1.0). High-CV absorbed features show 2x larger steering effect (0.153 vs 0.075). Rate-distortion theory explains variance paradox (733x CV ratio). Bypass vs mediated regime routing explains mechanism. Addresses Basu et al. actionability paradox by showing heterogeneity within absorbed features, not universal failure.
hypotheses: H1: High-CV absorbed features (above median CV within absorbed) show steering effects significantly larger than low-CV absorbed features (below median)
H4 (validated): Absorbed features have HIGHER CV than non-absorbed (CV=7.33 vs 0.01, 733x) - rate-distortion optimal preservation of high-variance info
Actionability paradox is not universal - some absorbed features (high-CV) remain steerable in non-clinical LLM domain
candidates.pilot_focus: 30 high-CV vs 30 low-CV steering comparison at +5 strength; Fano factor control for magnitude confounding; non-absorbed baseline comparison
strengths: Directly addresses field's key question (actionability paradox, Basu et al.)
First evidence that absorbed features are not uniformly non-steerable in non-clinical LLM domain
CV is simple statistical measure (no expensive steering needed to predict feasibility)
Training-free analysis of pretrained SAEs via SAELens
Rate-distortion theory provides principled explanation for variance paradox
Median CV split (prospective) addresses post-hoc threshold concern from contrarian
weaknesses: Steering effects are small in absolute terms (0.153 vs 0.075) - contrarian magnitude critique is valid
chi_ratio=1.88 < 3.0 undermines 'quasi-critical' phase transition framing (now supporting context only)
lambda_c instability (10x pilot-to-full shift) - critical point not fully reliable
H3 falsified at lambda=0.001 - cross-layer heterogeneity at lambda_c needs validation
CV threshold (1.0 replaced with median split) - still needs prospective validation on held-out features

theoretical_framework.primary: Rate-distortion theory (Shannon 1948) - high-variance features preserved because they carry unique reconstruction-critical information
theoretical_framework.secondary: Causal mediation framework (Pearl 2009) - bypass vs mediated regime routing explains differential steering effectiveness

candidates.title: Projection-Based Cross-Layer Absorption Quantification
candidates.summary: Use SAEBench probe projection metric (works across all layers, no ablation) to measure absorption at lambda_c=5e-5 where cross-layer heterogeneity may appear. Alternative if H3 retesting at lambda_c shows uniform saturation.
hypotheses: H3 (refined): Cross-layer absorption heterogeneity at lambda_c=5e-5
candidates.pilot_focus: Layers 0,3,6,9,11 at lambda_c=5e-5 using SAEBench probe projection metric
strengths: No ablation required - works across all layers
Publishable in either direction (variation found or not)
weaknesses: If no cross-layer variation found even at lambda_c, backup also fails
Still needs validation at true critical sparsity

candidates.title: Steering Effectiveness and Actionability Analysis
candidates.summary: Test whether absorption metrics predict steering effectiveness. Extends Basu et al. to non-clinical domain. If universal actionability failure confirmed, negative result is itself significant. If CV-based split works, confirms front-runner.
hypotheses: CV predicts steering effectiveness (high-CV absorbed features steerable)
candidates.pilot_focus: Steering test on 30 high-CV vs 30 low-CV absorbed features; absorbed vs non-absorbed steering comparison
strengths: Extends Basu et al. to non-clinical domain
Tests actionable hypothesis from CV discovery
weaknesses: If universal actionability failure confirmed, negative result may be too strong

candidates.title: Cross-Architecture Phase Transition Validation
candidates.summary: Validate that finite-size scaling (nu=3) discovered on GPT-2 TopK SAEs generalizes to Gemma-2-2B JumpReLU SAEs. Tests artifact hypothesis: are phase transitions GPT-2/TopK-specific or universal?
hypotheses: nu=3 is universal across SAE types
candidates.pilot_focus: GPT-2 sparsity sweep (confirm lambda_c=5e-5); Gemma-2 sparsity sweep (identify lambda_c); scaling collapse comparison
strengths: First cross-architecture validation of phase transition in SAEs
weaknesses: If Gemma-2 shows no phase transition, artifact hypothesis confirmed
lambda_c variation requires additional theoretical explanation

metadata.prior_feedback: Result debate verdict: 5.5/10, PIVOT recommendation. chi_ratio below threshold, H3/H6 falsified at lambda=0.001

activation_patching.interpretation: Genuine absorption confirmed - parent features recover substantially when child is zeroed

steering_cv.interpretation: High-CV features are 2x more steerable - CV predicts steering effectiveness

key_synthesis_decisions: CV-based actionability decomposition is the consensus front-runner across all 6 perspectives
Phase transition 'critical' framing downgraded to 'supporting theoretical context' due to chi_ratio=1.88 < 3.0
Rate-distortion theory (from interdisciplinary perspective) provides primary theoretical explanation for variance paradox
Causal mediation framework provides secondary mechanism explanation for bypass vs mediated regime routing
Median CV split (prospective) replaces post-hoc CV > 1.0 to address contrarian p-hacking concern
lambda_c instability acknowledged and framed as needing prospective validation
Cross-layer heterogeneity at lambda_c (H3) needs retesting - was falsified at wrong sparsity (lambda=0.001)
Contrarian magnitude critique acknowledged honestly: frame as 'small effect vs zero effect', not 'CV works vs doesn't work'
All 6 perspectives weighted roughly equally; innovator and empiricist perspectives validated the front-runner most strongly
